title: Fanout Pub/Sub and Subscriber Responsibilities · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go messaging team handles case 5806 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility, keep Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Late Subscriber Semantics
steps.instruction:

Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Late Subscriber Semantics structural check is only one gate. a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Late Subscriber Semantics, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Late Subscriber Semantics precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Late Subscriber Semantics, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go messaging team handles case 8280 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Late Subscriber Semantics, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A new fanout binding automatically receives messages published before it existed. In Go, multiple goroutines consuming one queue still implement competing consumers, not broadcast.

Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; retain screen-reader-readable topology and delivery evidence; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
